summary: Compiler OOM on large batch graphs
description: Compiling a graph with batch size above 512 reliably OOM kills the compiler process on our standard build hosts. Smaller batches compile fine.

comments.author: Jonah Kim
comments.body: Reproduced. The fusion pass holds intermediate tensors for the whole graph in memory at once before lowering, that's quadratic-ish in batch size for some fusion patterns.

comments.author: Sam Whitfield
comments.body: ack, will steer clear of large batch compiles until this is fixed

comments.author: Jonah Kim
comments.body: Fixed by streaming the fusion pass in chunks instead of holding the full graph. Verified batch 2048 compiles under the memory ceiling now. This does not affect anything in src/serving, purely a compiler-side change.

comments.author: Maya Okafor
comments.body: Great, thanks for the quick turnaround.
